bundle.description: Meta-package that installs every facade at once via exact-pinned "dependencies" and re-exposes all their bins.

description.description: npm package.json description for the bundle.

name.description: npm package name of the bundle.

facade.description:
A user-facing npm package exposing one binary. 
Each facade has its own per-platform sub-packages ("<scope>/<pkg>-<target.pkg>", one binary each) via "optionalDependencies".

alsoPublishAs.description: Additional npm names this facade is published under, byte-identical apart from "name" (e.g. a scoped twin of an unscoped facade).

bin.description: Binary this facade launches. Must be listed in "binaries".

description.description: npm package.json description for this facade.

name.description: npm package name users install. Unscoped or scoped.

pkg.description: Tool prefix for this facade's platform sub-packages: "<scope>/<pkg>-<target.pkg>".

shim.description: Unscoped alias package published alongside this facade: a thin package whose exact-pinned dependency and bin shims defer to the canonical (scoped) facade, so installs count on both names.

template.description: Template directory under "distribution/npm/facade/". Defaults to "name"; required when "name" is scoped (a "/" cannot appear in a directory name).

target.description:
Build target for a specific platform/configuration. 
Used to generate release tarballs and platform-specific sub-packages.

experimental.description: Used by the release workflow as "continue-on-error". Only valid on tier-3 targets.

libc.description:
npm "libc" field — only meaningful on Linux. 
Distinguishes glibc and musl builds so the right sub-package is installed on Alpine etc.

os.description:
npm "os" field — values from Node's "process.platform". 
npm uses this to select the correct "optionalDependency" at install time.

pkg.description: Platform identifier. Final package names are "<scope>/<facade.pkg>-<pkg>", one per facade.

rust.description:
Passed to "cargo build --target" / "cross build --target". 
Also used to locate the release tarball: "svg-<tag>-<rust>.tar.gz".

tier.description:
1 = first-class (release-blocking), 
2 = best-effort, 
3 = experimental (failures don't block release).

libc.description: libc is only meaningful on Linux. If declared, os must include linux.

then.description: Linux targets must declare libc to disambiguate glibc and musl builds at install time.
Only tier-3 targets may be marked experimental.

title: svg npm targets matrix
description: Build matrix for the svg facades and their shared per-platform optionalDependencies. Consumed by npm/scripts/build-packages.ts and the release workflow.

binaries.description:
Binary names extracted from each release tarball and shipped in every platform sub-package's "bin/" directory. 
On Windows, ".exe" is appended automatically.

facades.description:
User-facing npm packages, one per binary. 
Each resolves to the correct platform sub-package via "optionalDependencies".

grammars.description: Tree-sitter grammar node packages published to npm from "grammars/<name>/" at the release tag, version stamped from the tag and Bun "catalog:" references resolved at publish.

scope.description:
npm scope under which platform sub-packages are published. 
Each sub-package is published as "<scope>/<pkg>". Must be a scope the publishing npm account owns.

targets.description: Per-platform build targets. One entry per published sub-package.
